semanticCore: Intense hatred, detestation, abomination, or anger
quranicRelevance: God's severe hatred of hypocrisy or illicit marriage (Maqt - 'kabura maqtan 'inda Allah', 'innahu kana fahishatan wa maqtan').

keyDerivations.meaning: Intense hatred, severe detestation, abomination, or supreme anger

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَمَقْتُ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ مَقْتِكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ (40:10): Indeed, the hatred/anger of God is greater than your hatred of yourselves (judgment day remorse).
discardedNoise: Standard dictionary definitions of step-mother marriages (نكاح المقت) in details outside Surah an-Nisa.
summaryEn: The root denotes severe detestation (Maqt), warning against talking without doing, condemning pre-Islamic stepmother marriages, and detailing judgment day self-loathing.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على أشد البغض والكره (مقت)، محذراً من القول بلا عمل، ومحسناً تحريم الزواج بزوجات الآباء، ومصوراً غضب الرب يوم الحساب.
rawText: تماقتوا 6 They hated one another, [or hated one another in the utmost degree,] on account of some foul, or evil, affair. (A.) تمقّت إِلَيْهِ 5 He showed, or manifested, hatred [or the utmost hatred] to him: contr. of تحبّب اليه. (A.) مَا أَمْقَتَهُ عِنْدِى 4 How hated, or hateful, or odious, is he to me! (Sb, K.) === مَا أَمْقَتَنِى لَهُ How I hate him! (Sb, K.) مَاقَتَهُ 3 He hated him [or hated him in the utmost degree] reciprocally, on account of some foul, or evil, affair. (A.) === مَاقَتُوا [They bore hatred, one to another.] (TA, art. بهت.) مَقَتَهُ 1 , (aor. ﹹ, Msb,) inf. n. مَقْتٌ (and, accord. to the K, مَقَاتَةٌ; but this is the inf. n. of مَقُتَ, q. v.; TA) He hated him: (S, K:) as also 🡻مقّته, (K,) inf. n. تَمْقِيتٌ; (TA;) [but this has, probably, an intensive signification;] or he hated him on account of a foul action that he had committed: (Lth:) or he hated him with the most violent hatred, (Zj, M, Er-Rághib, Msb,) on account of a foul action. (Msb.) === مَقُتَ إِلَى النَّاسِ, aor. ﹹ, inf. n. مَقَاتَةٌ, He was hateful, or an object of hatred, to men, on account of a foul action that he had committed: (Lth:) or he was hateful, or an object of hatred, in the utmost degree, (M, Msb,) on account of a foul action. (Msb.) مَقِيتٌ and 🡻مَمْقُوتٌ Hated: (S, K:) or the former signifies hateful, and the latter hated: (MF:) [or hateful, and hated, on account of his having committed a foul action: in this sense مقيت is mentioned by Lth: or hateful, and hated, in the utmost degree, on account of a foul action: in this sense مقيت is mentioned in the Msb.] مَقَّتَ 2 see 1. مَقْتِىًّ A man who has contracted the kind of marriage termed نكاح المقت: (ISd, K:) or the son of such a man. (Zj, K.) مَمْقُوتٌ : see مَقِيتٌ. نِكَاحُ المَقْتِ The marrying the wife of one's father after him; (K;) that is, after his having divorced her or died: (TA:) this was done in the time of paganism, (S,) and was then thus called; مَقْتٌ signifying the “ most violent hatred: ” it is forbidden in the Kur, iv. 26. (Zj.)
